method.official_sources: Official GitHub README, docs, or product pages only. No third-party benchmark claims were used for positioning.

entries.positioning: Universal memory layer for AI agents with user/session/agent memory, library, self-hosted server, cloud platform, CLI, skills, and multi-signal retrieval claims.
Memory and context layer with app, plugins, MCP, API, user profiles, connectors, file processing, project/container tag scoping, and Claude Code/OpenCode plugin claims.

Persistent memory compression for Claude Code with auto-capture, summaries, future-session injection, worker service, SQLite, Chroma, MCP search tools, and documented Gemini CLI/OpenCode install paths.
Local-first Markdown knowledge system built on MCP with local notes, assistant context loading, VS Code and Claude Desktop setup, and local/cloud project routing.
Self-hosted shared memory backend for AI agent pipelines with REST API, MCP, OAuth, CLI, dashboard, knowledge graph, autonomous consolidation, and multiple agent framework integrations.
Open-source temporal context graph engine and Zep context infrastructure with validity windows, hybrid semantic/keyword/graph retrieval, and MCP server.
Stateful agent platform and Letta Code local terminal agent with advanced memory, local agent execution, and git-backed MemFS/context repositories.

OS-level/local developer workflow memory product with LTM-2, MCP access, GitHub Copilot/Cursor/Goose integrations, source/time-based recall, and local privacy claims. Stars/license are for the official docs repo, not the closed product core.

caveats: This snapshot does not install or benchmark any competitor.
Stars and license values are point-in-time GitHub API metadata.
For Pieces, no core product repository/license was identified in the official GitHub organization during this pass; the audited GitHub repo is the official documentation repository.
Claims in README should remain narrower than this market landscape: local coding-session continuity for supported Claude Code and Codex paths, not general memory superiority.
